skill.name: code-to-music
skill.description: Create original instrumental music with code using music21, mido, FluidSynth, and pydub. The skill guides MIDI composition, instrument mapping, MP3 rendering, and audio quality validation for acoustic and electronic styles.
skill.summary: Create original instrumental MP3 music from code-driven MIDI compositions.
skill.author: Cam10001110101

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported many high and critical patterns, but review found they are mostly false positives from music terminology, MIDI program names, and documentation examples. No evidence found of credential access, C2 behavior, data exfiltration, or prompt injection. The remaining publishable risk is that the community installer runs apt and pip package installation and the scripts create local audio files.

medium_findings.title: Installer Runs System and Python Package Installation
medium_findings.description: The installer runs apt-get update, apt-get install, pip upgrade, and pip install from requirements.txt. This is expected for audio rendering dependencies, but it modifies the host environment and downloads third-party packages for a community skill.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The commands are directly present in the installer and requirements file. They are legitimate setup actions, but package installation is a real supply-chain and host-modification risk.

low_findings.title: Static Critical and High Music-Term False Positives
low_findings.description: Terms flagged as C2, Windows SAM, and weak cryptography appear in music context, such as MIDI channel guidance, chord and octave labels, soundfont wording, and program numbers. No evidence found that these terms refer to command-and-control, credential files, or cryptographic behavior.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The reviewed lines are clearly music instructions, not security-sensitive operations. The suspicious labels come from keyword collisions in normal music terminology.

low_findings.title: Documentation URLs Without Runtime Network Calls
low_findings.description: The skill lists external documentation URLs for music21, MIDI, music theory, and IMSLP. These are references only; no code path was found that sends audio, prompts, credentials, or local data to those sites.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The lines contain plain markdown links, and targeted search found no HTTP client usage in executable scripts. This supports a false-positive network assessment.

low_findings.title: Local Audio File Writes and Cleanup
low_findings.description: The rendering and MIDI utility scripts create MIDI, WAV, MP3, or derived MIDI files and remove temporary files. These actions are local and tied to user-provided input or output paths, with no evidence of hidden file collection or exfiltration.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The file operations are visible and match the stated audio workflow. Residual risk remains because user-selected output paths can write files locally.

dangerous_patterns.title: Package Manager Execution in Installer
dangerous_patterns.description: The installer invokes apt-get and pip commands. This is not malicious by itself, but users should review installer behavior before running it because it changes the environment.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The package manager commands are explicit. The risk is operational and supply-chain related, not evidence of malicious intent.

dangerous_patterns.title: User-Path File Creation and Temporary File Removal
dangerous_patterns.description: The renderer derives MIDI and WAV paths from the requested MP3 output and removes those temporary files after export. This is normal for rendering, but it should stay constrained to intended output locations.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The code clearly creates and deletes local render artifacts. It is legitimate behavior, but it touches the filesystem based on user-selected paths.

content.value_statement: Turning a musical idea into a finished audio file requires composition, MIDI setup, rendering, and validation. This skill guides Claude, Codex, and Claude Code through original instrumental music generation and MP3 export.
actual_capabilities: Guide original instrumental composition with music21 patterns and music theory references.
Select traditional or electronic rendering guidance based on genre and instrument requests.
Set MIDI program numbers and velocities with mido to control instrument sound and balance.
Render MIDI structures to MP3 files with FluidSynth and pydub.
Validate generated audio for clipping, silence, duration, loudness, and dynamic range.
Extract and transform MIDI structure for local analysis and remix workflows.
limitations: It is for original instrumental music, not vocals, lyrics, or sound-alike copies of protected songs.
Output quality depends on available soundfonts and local audio dependencies.
It renders files to disk and does not provide real-time playback.
It is not a full mixing or mastering environment for professional studio production.

use_cases.title: Prototype Game Music
use_cases.description: Generate short looping themes, ambient beds, or scene-specific instrumental tracks for rapid game iteration.

use_cases.title: Create Video Background Tracks
use_cases.description: Produce original MP3 music for explainers, demos, and social videos without relying on stock audio.

use_cases.title: Explore Composition by Code
use_cases.description: Test chord progressions, instrument ranges, rhythm patterns, and arrangement ideas through repeatable scripts.

prompt_templates.title: Simple Piano Theme
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner request for a short original melody.
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a simple original piano theme in C major at 100 BPM. Make it calm, 30 seconds long, and export it as piano_theme.mp3.

prompt_templates.title: Electronic Loop
prompt_templates.scenario: Intermediate request for an electronic groove.
prompt_templates.prompt: Create an original deep house loop with synth bass, pads, kick, claps, and shakers. Use a 124 BPM groove and export an MP3.

prompt_templates.title: Orchestral Sketch
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced request for a multi-part arrangement.
prompt_templates.prompt: Compose a one-minute original orchestral sketch with strings, brass, woodwinds, and percussion. Include a clear intro, build, climax, and ending.

prompt_templates.title: Validate and Refine Audio
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced request for quality review after rendering.
prompt_templates.prompt: Render the composition to MP3, validate it for clipping and silence, then adjust velocities or arrangement if the validation reports problems.

output_examples.input: Create a peaceful ambient track with soft pads and no drums.
output: A local MP3 file with layered synth pads and slow harmonic movement.
A spacious arrangement with long note durations and a gentle fade-out.
Audio validation notes for duration, peak level, and silence percentage.

output_examples.input: Make an upbeat electronic dance track for a short demo reel.
output: A rendered MP3 with kick, claps, shakers, syncopated synth bass, and lead accents.
Instrument and velocity choices tuned for a balanced electronic mix.
A structured arrangement with intro, full groove, breakdown, and return.

output_examples.input: Write a short string quartet idea with a dramatic ending.
output: An original chamber-style MP3 using violin, viola, and cello ranges.
A musical structure with theme statement, development, and final cadence.
Local MIDI and audio rendering steps suitable for later revision.

best_practices: Start with a clear genre, tempo, duration, mood, and target instruments.
Use mido program changes and explicit velocities after MIDI export.
Run audio validation after rendering and revise dynamics before delivery.
anti_patterns: Asking for a copy of an existing song, beat, riff, or protected musical style imitation.
Relying on default MIDI velocity and program settings for final audio quality.
Skipping local dependency review before running the community install script.

faq.question: What does this skill create?
faq.answer: It creates original instrumental music as local MIDI and MP3 audio files.

faq.question: Can it create vocals or lyrics?
faq.answer: No. The skill is designed for instrumental MIDI-based music, not sung vocals or lyric generation.

faq.question: Does it copy existing songs?
faq.answer: No. It explicitly focuses on original compositions and avoids replicating protected songs, riffs, or beats.

faq.question: Which tools can use it?
faq.answer: The report lists support for Claude, Codex, and Claude Code.

faq.question: Does it require local dependencies?
faq.answer: Yes. Rendering depends on Python audio packages, FluidSynth, FFmpeg, and soundfont files.

faq.question: Does it send audio to external services?
faq.answer: No evidence found of runtime network upload. The reviewed scripts process files locally.
